# Styleguide — rhino-os v3.0

rhino-os is a CLI/configuration system. There is no web UI. The "design system" is terminal output conventions and file format standards.

---

## Terminal Palette

Defined in `bin/rhino` (ANSI escape codes):

| Name | Code | Usage |
|------|------|-------|
| RED | `\033[0;31m` | Errors, FAIL, RED triage, integrity warnings |
| GREEN | `\033[0;32m` | PASS, GREEN triage, kept experiments |
| YELLOW | `\033[1;33m` | Warnings, YELLOW triage, pending items |
| BLUE | `\033[0;34m` | Headers, section titles |
| BOLD | `\033[1m` | Emphasis, score numbers |
| DIM | `\033[2m` | Secondary info, timestamps, paths |
| NC | `\033[0m` | Reset |

### Output Conventions

- **Score display**: `BOLD` number + dimension breakdown
- **Integrity warnings**: `YELLOW` prefix + warning text
- **Pass/Fail**: `GREEN` PASS / `RED` FAIL
- **History trends**: `GREEN` ↑ / `RED` ↓ / `DIM` →
- **Section headers**: `BLUE` + `BOLD`

---

## File Format Standards

### TSV Files

All TSV files use tab separation, first row is header, no quoting unless value contains tabs.

| File | Columns |
|------|---------|
| `scores/history.tsv` | timestamp, build, structure, hygiene, project_type |
| `experiments/*.tsv` | commit, score, delta, status, description, learning |
| `evals/taste-history.tsv` | timestamp, overall_score, [11 dimensions], weakest_dimension, feature |
| `predictions.tsv` | date, agent, prediction, evidence, result, correct, model_update |
| `patterns.tsv` | pattern, type, count, first_seen, last_seen, status |
| `cost-history.tsv` | date, project, tool_calls, edit_count, read_count, bash_count, agent_count, duration_min |

### JSON Files

All JSON is pretty-printed (2-space indent) for human readability. No minification.

- Brain files: 2-space indent, string values for next_move/bias
- workspace.json: 2-space indent, nested project objects
- score-cache.json: 2-space indent, flat structure with `integrity_warnings` array

### JSONL Files

One JSON object per line, no pretty-printing. Used for append-only logs:
- `usage.jsonl`, `grades.jsonl`, `artifact-failures.jsonl`, `history.jsonl`

### Markdown Files

- Metadata comment on line 1: `<!-- Generated by [source] | [ISO date] | [git SHA] -->`
- H1 for document title, H2 for major sections, H3 for subsections
- Code blocks with language hints where applicable
- Tables for structured data (pipes + dashes)

### YAML Files

- `rhino.yml`: 2-space indent, comments for documentation, `~` for null/unlimited
- `features.yml`: 2-space indent, dash-prefix lists for routes
- `taste.yml`: 2-space indent, auth config only

---

## Naming Conventions

### Files

- Skills: `skills/{name}/SKILL.md` (lowercase kebab-case directory, uppercase SKILL.md)
- Programs: `programs/{name}.md` (lowercase)
- Hooks: `hooks/{name}.sh` (snake_case)
- Brains: `brains/{agent-name}.json` (kebab-case matching agent name)
- Experiments: `experiments/{dimension}.tsv` (lowercase dimension name)
- Eval reports: `evals/reports/{feature}-{YYYY-MM-DD}.md` or `taste-{timestamp}.json`
- Score history: `scores/history.tsv` (singular)

### Agent Names

6 agents, kebab-case: `strategist`, `builder`, `sweep`, `scout`, `design-engineer`, `meta`

### Config Keys

Dotted path in rhino.yml: `scoring.build.gate_threshold`, `taste.max_routes`, `integrity.stage_ceilings.mvp`

Read via `cfg "key"` function in config.sh.

---

## Score Display Format

### Visual Mode (default)

```
═══════════════════════════════════════
  rhino score · project-name
═══════════════════════════════════════

  BUILD       100  ██████████  PASS
  STRUCTURE    70  ███████···
  HYGIENE      70  ███████···

  SCORE        70  ███████···

  ⚠ PLATEAU: structure unchanged across last 5 runs

  History: 68 → 70 → 70 → 70 → 70

═══════════════════════════════════════
```

### JSON Mode

Single-line JSON object with all fields.

### Quiet Mode

Single number: `70`

---

## Taste Display Format

```
═══════════════════════════════════════
  rhino taste · project-name
═══════════════════════════════════════

  hierarchy           3/5  ███·····
  breathing_room      2/5  ██······
  contrast            3/5  ███·····
  polish              3/5  ███·····
  emotional_tone      2/5  ██······
  information_density 3/5  ███·····
  wayfinding          2/5  ██······
  distinctiveness     2/5  ██······
  scroll_experience   2/5  ██······
  layout_coherence    1/5  █·······  ← GATE
  information_arch    2/5  ██······  ← GATE

  OVERALL: 2.2/5 (MARGINAL)

  Weakest: layout_coherence
  One thing: Cards are inconsistent sizes. Fix max-width to 320px.
  Would return: Probably not
  Would recommend: No

═══════════════════════════════════════
```

---

## Commit Message Format

Via `/smart-commit`:

```
type(scope): description

[optional body]
```

Types: `feat`, `fix`, `refactor`, `chore`, `docs`, `test`, `style`

Scope: derived from active plan task or changed files.

No `Co-Authored-By` lines (per founder rule).
